name: Turkey with Southwest Stuffing
recipeIngredient: 4 Jalapeno Chiles;  (Seed and finely chop chiles)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare Southwest Stuffing.
2. Fill the wishbone area of the turkey with stuffing.
3. Fasten the neck skin to the back using a skewer.
4. Fold the wings across the back, touching at the tips.
5. Lightly fill the body cavity with stuffing (do not pack as it will expand).
6. Tuck the drumsticks under the band of skin at the tail or secure with a skewer.
7. Spoon any remaining stuffing into a small ungreased casserole; cover.
- Refrigerate leftover stuffing for 30 minutes before baking.
8. Heat oven to 325 degrees F (°C).
9. Place turkey breast side up in a roasting pan.
10. Brush with melted butter.
11. Insert a meat thermometer into the thigh muscle or breast, not touching the bone.
- The tip can also be inserted into the center of the stuffing.
12. Do not add water and do not cover the turkey.
13. Roast until done (3½ to 4 hours).
14. Place a loose tent of aluminum foil over the turkey when it begins to turn golden.
15. After 2½ hours, cut or remove the skewer holding the legs.
16. Turkey is done when the thermometer in the thigh muscle registers 185 degrees F and drumstick meat feels very soft when pressed between fingers (the stuffing will register 165 degrees F).
17. Let stand about 20 minutes before carving.
18. As soon as possible after serving, remove every bit of stuffing from the turkey.
19. Cool the stuffing and turkey promptly; refrigerate separately and use within 2 days.
